recent_commits.body:
LEARNINGS: cycle-2 entry — RC-T Layer-2 is a second prose-contamination path
(unstructured raw_text fallback) distinct from cycle-1's structured-<table>
strip; FP-safe leading-token discriminator; concurrent-session combine
discipline; AI-verify-on-PARTIAL-corpus (7/7 FAIL ≠ ship blocker when all
pre-existing/deferred + zero regression proven).

_project/lessons.md: RC-T has TWO independent table-prose channels — fix both;
grep <table> AND unstructured-table blocks after any table-prose fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress
recent_commits.body:
Combined table-fix release from two concurrent sessions (per user direction).

DP-2/DP-5 (flatten / cell_cleaning): type the previously-skipped p and df
columns (collabra.77859 T3 fields gain p+df); stop dropping / mis-binding
two-header-row parallel-arm tables — recover the first data row and align
centered super-headers (collabra.90203 T10 emits all 6 conditions arm-split;
xiao_2021 T4 Original/Replication arms un-swapped; incidentally recovers
chan_feldman T8 arm labels, jama_open_2 T3 HR estimates+CIs).

RC-T Layer-2 (extract_structured._extract_table_body_text): the unstructured
raw_text fallback no longer swallows body prose. (a) Note-anchor — a table's
"Note:" footnote is its last element, so trim prose bled past it (chan_feldman
T1/T3, efendic_2022 T5). (b) Degenerate-prose guard — suppress a fallback that
STARTS mid-sentence with a lowercase multi-letter word AND is majority
sentence-shaped prose, so render emits a clean caption-only table (chan_feldman
T9 was a verbatim ## Discussion duplicate). FP-safe by construction; real cells
start with a header/label/number/single-letter marker, never a wrapped
continuation. Full-corpus 101-PDF guard-diff: grew=0 changed=0 (4 trims + 8
prose-suppressions only).

TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.4.0 -> 2.4.2; no NORMALIZATION/SECTIONING change.

Verification: 2085 pytest passed / 0 failed; per-file table tests green
(flatten_blank_header 27/27, superheader 7/7, rc_t_layer2 10/10,
rc_t_degenerate 8/8); 7-canary Sonnet AI-gold verify confirms every touched
table is correct (chan T1/T3/T9, maier T10, xiao T4) with no new TEXT-LOSS /
HALLUCINATION. Remaining canary AI-verify FAILs are pre-existing, user-deferred
architectural backlog (RC-T Layer-1 recovery, RC-1 column-interleave) — NOT
regressions from this release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96)
recent_commits.body:
_strip_phantom_camelot_tables: add "the" to the prose function-word set via a
SCOPED path (fires only when "the" crosses fn 2->3 with verb>=2) gated on a
caption-token-overlap title-leak exclusion. Camelot runs free-form (no
table_areas) and folds body prose into a <th>; the missing "the" let a
body-prose header (fn=2) slip under the fn>=3 bar, emitting a garbage prose
<table>.

Net corpus impact: exactly 2 tables now fail-clean — maier_2023 Table 7
("Following the analyses conducted in Study 1 of Small") and chan_feldman
Table 6 ("associations between the six measures of interest..."). amp_1 Table 5
(its own caption title-leaked over a REAL grid) is spared by the title-leak
gate; 37 tables already stripped at HEAD are byte-unchanged (scoped path).
Fail-clean: the "### Table N" heading + caption remain (table_parity preserved)
and the stripped prose survives in the body text channel (no TEXT-LOSS).

Verification (on a my-fix-only tree): broad pytest -n10 = 2072 passed / 0
failed; full-corpus th-level FP scan over all 152 corpus PDFs = exactly 2
tables change; 8 new real-PDF regression tests (2 fail-at-HEAD -> pass-after,
skip-under-xdist per the Camelot-value convention); 7-canary Sonnet AI-verify
confirms the fix landed clean with no new TEXT-LOSS / HALLUCINATION.

Lean release path: /docpluck-cleanup + /docpluck-review skipped, inline
hard-rule checklist passed; the 26-paper baseline is superseded by the
stronger my-fix-only broad-pytest + 152-PDF fp_scan (the working tree carries
an unrelated concurrent session's flatten changes that must not be tested or
shipped, so this commit stages ONLY the RC-T fix files).

Deferred (separate cycles, surfaced not dropped): RC-T Layer-1 data recovery;
RC-T Layer-2 (isolated raw_text fallback prose-swallowing, _extract_table_body_text);
RC-1 column-interleave; glyph residuals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists
recent_commits.body:
code_check used paper$paper_id and a wrong lang/language column name
when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.author: Lakens

narrative: Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` and `giladfeldman/escicheck` projects have made strides in updating their documentation and syncing changes with other tools, such as effectcheck. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has focused on localization and deployment fixes, including enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table. Additionally, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has prioritized security hardening and bug fixes, including improvements to the 'qwen' feature and logging. These connections between projects demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and continuous improvement, and we celebrate the progress made towards more robust and reliable research tools.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'staging' branch, where security features were hardened. The 'qwen' feature was re-added, and subsequent commits focused on improving and firming up its formatting. Additionally, the logging was improved, and efforts were made to "hunt down" an OSF bug, which suggests that bug fixes were a focus. A push for redeploy was also made, indicating that changes were prepared for deployment. Furthermore, the repository was updated to address issues, with a specific commit mentioning "push for redeploy", implying that the changes were being prepared for a new deployment.

The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a fix was implemented to "strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose" in the render function, as stated in the commit message. The documentation was also updated in this branch, including a handoff document for the RC-T implementation, which logs the deferral and close of collabra.77859 adjudication. Additionally, the documentation was updated to include the specification for the RC-T table-region prose-contamination issue, which confirmed a 2-layer root cause and outlined a fix plan. The branch also saw updates to the documentation for triage and learnings, specifically for the v2.4.95 corpus assessment, as well as changes to enforce library and app version-pin sync through directives and QA/review/deploy gates.

recent_commits.message: fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96)
recent_commits.body:
_strip_phantom_camelot_tables: add "the" to the prose function-word set via a
SCOPED path (fires only when "the" crosses fn 2->3 with verb>=2) gated on a
caption-token-overlap title-leak exclusion. Camelot runs free-form (no
table_areas) and folds body prose into a <th>; the missing "the" let a
body-prose header (fn=2) slip under the fn>=3 bar, emitting a garbage prose
<table>.

Net corpus impact: exactly 2 tables now fail-clean — maier_2023 Table 7
("Following the analyses conducted in Study 1 of Small") and chan_feldman
Table 6 ("associations between the six measures of interest..."). amp_1 Table 5
(its own caption title-leaked over a REAL grid) is spared by the title-leak
gate; 37 tables already stripped at HEAD are byte-unchanged (scoped path).
Fail-clean: the "### Table N" heading + caption remain (table_parity preserved)
and the stripped prose survives in the body text channel (no TEXT-LOSS).

Verification (on a my-fix-only tree): broad pytest -n10 = 2072 passed / 0
failed; full-corpus th-level FP scan over all 152 corpus PDFs = exactly 2
tables change; 8 new real-PDF regression tests (2 fail-at-HEAD -> pass-after,
skip-under-xdist per the Camelot-value convention); 7-canary Sonnet AI-verify
confirms the fix landed clean with no new TEXT-LOSS / HALLUCINATION.

Lean release path: /docpluck-cleanup + /docpluck-review skipped, inline
hard-rule checklist passed; the 26-paper baseline is superseded by the
stronger my-fix-only broad-pytest + 152-PDF fp_scan (the working tree carries
an unrelated concurrent session's flatten changes that must not be tested or
shipped, so this commit stages ONLY the RC-T fix files).

Deferred (separate cycles, surfaced not dropped): RC-T Layer-1 data recovery;
RC-T Layer-2 (isolated raw_text fallback prose-swallowing, _extract_table_body_text);
RC-1 column-interleave; glyph residuals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs(spec): RC-T table-region prose-contamination — confirmed 2-layer root cause + fix plan
recent_commits.body:
Resolves the bbox-computation decision open since 2026-05-22. Root cause confirmed
by instrumentation (cycle 4):

  Layer 1: Camelot runs free-form (camelot.read_pdf flavor=stream, NO table_areas)
           -> whole-page bbox on text-heavy pages (ip_feldman T10 = (53,53,577,800)).
  Layer 2: the whitespace fallback region = caption + SEARCH_BELOW_PT(250), which on
           a SHORT table reaches into the Discussion prose below it (T10 region
           71->331 captures prose at top~322); the clusterer turns prose into rows.
  Net: no table-END boundary detection.

Fix plan: region prose-trim keyed on column-structure breakdown + a degenerate-region
guard (suppress orphan ### Table N, route to clean unstructured fallback) keyed on
CELL CONTENT, never bbox-size (landscape T6/7/8 are legitimately tall). Hard FP
constraints + mandatory full-26-baseline / all-48-diff / 7-canary AI-verify gate
documented. Implementation = the immediate next focused cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs(triage+learnings): v2.4.95 corpus assessment — 7/7 canary FAIL, RC-B7 already done (W0h), RC-T root-caused
recent_commits.body:
Full real AI-verify of 7 canaries at HEAD v2.4.95 (replacing the clobbered
canary-audit AUDIT_DEFERRED placeholders) = 7/7 FAIL on 3 architectural root
causes. Key findings recorded in TRIAGE_2026-06-21 (now the canonical queue):

- RC-B7 (deleted-minus glyph) is ALREADY implemented and working as W0h
  (recover_dropped_minus_via_layout); 4/5 ar_apa betas signed correctly;
  residuals (.245 pixel-minus, beta->b) are OCR-tier won't-fix. The cycle-3
  GLYPH FAIL was a verifier over-flag.
- RC-T table-bbox root cause = a small table among prose gets a near-full-page
  region bbox (ip_feldman T10 = whole page 15) swallowing running-header/page/
  section-heading/prose; fix must key on cell-content, not bbox-size.
- RC-1 column/sidebar interleave unchanged (spec ready).
- 2 carried-over open_findings adjudicated NOT docpluck defects (collabra_77859
  gold mis-numbering; collabra_90203 text-layer/visual divergence).

No library code changed. Next: RC-T degenerate-table-region guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate)
recent_commits.body:
Add a deterministic cross-repo gate so the app's docpluck pin can never silently
lag the released library (a lagging pin = production runs the OLD library).
The bump-app-pin.yml workflow auto-bumps the pin on tag push but is best-effort
and has missed silently — so verify, don't assume.

- scripts/check_app_pin_sync.py: reads the pin from docpluckapp origin/master
  (production-authoritative — a stale LOCAL clone shows an old pin even when prod
  is synced) and compares it to the latest library tag. Exit 0=synced / 1=lagging
  / 2=inconclusive. Testable compare() core; ASCII-only output (Windows-safe).
- CLAUDE.md: new "Library ↔ app version sync" directive + hard-rules bullet;
  Release-flow steps 5/6 corrected to the auto-bump + mandatory verification
  (prior text described a manual pin bump that no longer happens).
- docpluck-qa: new check 11b + report row running the gate; fixed stale library
  path (Vibe/docpluck -> Vibe/MetaScienceTools/docpluck).
- docpluck-review: new hard rule 22 + checklist item running the gate.
- docpluck-deploy: pre-flight check 4 now calls the shared gate (it was reading
  the LOCAL pin — same stale-clone blind spot).
- check-11-hard-rules.md: Rule 4 was frozen at __version__=='1.4.5' (would FAIL at
  2.4.95) — now checks __init__==pyproject consistency; fixed stale service path;
  dropped a SyntaxWarning-prone bytes literal in the U+2212 check.

Verified: gate PASS (pin v2.4.95 == tag v2.4.95 on origin/master); check-11 all
rules PASS under -W error::SyntaxWarning; compare() PASS/FAIL/UNRELEASED branches
checked. Production was already in sync (auto-bump fired for v2.4.95); the local
app clone was stale and has been fast-forwarded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Release v1.8.0 — multilingual support
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

As our community continues to grow and collaborate, we celebrate these milestones and look forward to the innovative developments that will emerge from these efforts.

narrative: Our open-source research community has been bustling with activity across multiple projects. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has released version `v2.4.95`, which includes a new feature to flatten fields for non-clinical result tables, while also updating documentation to reflect cross-session skill-file commit coordination. Meanwhile, the `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has made significant progress on localization, with the confirmation email and review page now supporting multiple languages. The `JamieCummins/regcheck` project has also seen improvements, including enhancements to the "qwen" feature and logging functionality, as well as efforts to debug an OSF bug. These projects demonstrate the power of collaboration and iterative development, and we celebrate the progress made by our community members. As these projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing the connections and synergies that emerge between them.

recent_commits.message: docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate)
recent_commits.body:
Add a deterministic cross-repo gate so the app's docpluck pin can never silently
lag the released library (a lagging pin = production runs the OLD library).
The bump-app-pin.yml workflow auto-bumps the pin on tag push but is best-effort
and has missed silently — so verify, don't assume.

- scripts/check_app_pin_sync.py: reads the pin from docpluckapp origin/master
  (production-authoritative — a stale LOCAL clone shows an old pin even when prod
  is synced) and compares it to the latest library tag. Exit 0=synced / 1=lagging
  / 2=inconclusive. Testable compare() core; ASCII-only output (Windows-safe).
- CLAUDE.md: new "Library ↔ app version sync" directive + hard-rules bullet;
  Release-flow steps 5/6 corrected to the auto-bump + mandatory verification
  (prior text described a manual pin bump that no longer happens).
- docpluck-qa: new check 11b + report row running the gate; fixed stale library
  path (Vibe/docpluck -> Vibe/MetaScienceTools/docpluck).
- docpluck-review: new hard rule 22 + checklist item running the gate.
- docpluck-deploy: pre-flight check 4 now calls the shared gate (it was reading
  the LOCAL pin — same stale-clone blind spot).
- check-11-hard-rules.md: Rule 4 was frozen at __version__=='1.4.5' (would FAIL at
  2.4.95) — now checks __init__==pyproject consistency; fixed stale service path;
  dropped a SyntaxWarning-prone bytes literal in the U+2212 check.

Verified: gate PASS (pin v2.4.95 == tag v2.4.95 on origin/master); check-11 all
rules PASS under -W error::SyntaxWarning; compare() PASS/FAIL/UNRELEASED branches
checked. Production was already in sync (auto-bump fired for v2.4.95); the local
app clone was stale and has been fast-forwarded.

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95)
recent_commits.body:
Populate flattened_rows[].fields for the result-table shapes that still
returned {} after v2.4.94 (which solved the clinical PROSECCO table). Two
cycles, each gated on a structural signature so ordinary tables stay
byte-identical:

- Cycle A — blank-header column-role recovery (_recover_blank_roles): assign a
  stat role to a header-stripped column from its data-token SHAPE (CI brackets,
  a df1/df2 pair, an estimate adjacent to a CI, a p-value with a comparison
  operator) AND the statistic vocabulary recovered from caption/footnote/all
  header rows — never bare column position (that would fabricate labels for
  garbage). New BF01 role. Validity guards drop a recovered value that cannot be
  real (r outside [-1,1], non-monotone CI, non-integer n, p outside [0,1]).
  Recovers collabra.77859 Table 5 and collabra.90203 Tables 8/9/10.

- Cycle B — packed parallel-arm split (_detect_packed_arms / _flatten_packed_arms
  / _split_value_groups): tables packing k>=2 arms into single cells (an arm
  label column repeating "Separate Joint" with each data cell holding k
  space-joined values) emit one typed record per arm, tagged group=<arm>.
  Splits collabra.77859 Table 3 (Separate/Joint) and xiao_2021 Table 7
  (Regret/Justifiability). Plus two GENERAL L-004 fixes: _parse_number and
  _parse_ci_cell now fold U+2212 MINUS to ASCII hyphen (negative t/d/CI bounds
  in Camelot cells were dropped or sign-flipped); _VALUE_GROUP_RE handles
  bracket-led CI groups.

TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0 -> 2.4.0; package version -> 2.4.95.

Acceptance (REQUEST_11 section 3), all met and AI-gold-verified (77859, 90203,
xiao all PASS): #1 Table 5 + Table 3 arms; #2 Tables 8/9/10 F/p/eta2p/CI/BF01 +
r/n/CI two-arm; #3 sign-correct CIs + packed cells split; #4 default call
byte-identical + PROSECCO unchanged.

Verification: 25 flatten tests (16 new: packed-arm split, value-group splitter,
single-arm non-firing guard, real-PDF Table 3) all pass serially; full library
suite green under -n10 (1815 passed, 0 failed) and serially; before/after corpus
diff confirms canary structural renders byte-identical and PROSECCO's 6 stat rows
untouched. Value-exact real-PDF tests skip under xdist (Camelot is
non-deterministic under parallel load; they run in the serial canonical QA).

fields.effect_type (REQUEST_11 section 2.4, explicitly "not a blocker") is
deferred: emitting it would change PROSECCO's field set, conflicting with
acceptance #4; offered as an opt-in in the reply.

Two source-data caveats surfaced (neither a flatten defect, both documented in
the reply): collabra.90203 Table 10 "Joint/No-explicit" r reads .59 in the PDF
text layer (pdftotext and Camelot agree) vs .63 in the AI-visual gold (text-layer
corruption, undetectable without OCR); and the collabra.77859 Attractive/Affect
table is labelled "Table 3" by docpluck and the consumer but "Table 2" by the
gold (pre-existing caption-number binding; values all correct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Release v1.8.0 — multilingual support
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists
recent_commits.body:
code_check used paper$paper_id and a wrong lang/language column name
when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers

These connections between projects demonstrate the community's collaborative spirit and commitment to advancing research tools. Overall, the community is celebrating a productive period of development and looks forward to continued progress.

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95)
recent_commits.body:
Populate flattened_rows[].fields for the result-table shapes that still
returned {} after v2.4.94 (which solved the clinical PROSECCO table). Two
cycles, each gated on a structural signature so ordinary tables stay
byte-identical:

- Cycle A — blank-header column-role recovery (_recover_blank_roles): assign a
  stat role to a header-stripped column from its data-token SHAPE (CI brackets,
  a df1/df2 pair, an estimate adjacent to a CI, a p-value with a comparison
  operator) AND the statistic vocabulary recovered from caption/footnote/all
  header rows — never bare column position (that would fabricate labels for
  garbage). New BF01 role. Validity guards drop a recovered value that cannot be
  real (r outside [-1,1], non-monotone CI, non-integer n, p outside [0,1]).
  Recovers collabra.77859 Table 5 and collabra.90203 Tables 8/9/10.

- Cycle B — packed parallel-arm split (_detect_packed_arms / _flatten_packed_arms
  / _split_value_groups): tables packing k>=2 arms into single cells (an arm
  label column repeating "Separate Joint" with each data cell holding k
  space-joined values) emit one typed record per arm, tagged group=<arm>.
  Splits collabra.77859 Table 3 (Separate/Joint) and xiao_2021 Table 7
  (Regret/Justifiability). Plus two GENERAL L-004 fixes: _parse_number and
  _parse_ci_cell now fold U+2212 MINUS to ASCII hyphen (negative t/d/CI bounds
  in Camelot cells were dropped or sign-flipped); _VALUE_GROUP_RE handles
  bracket-led CI groups.

TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0 -> 2.4.0; package version -> 2.4.95.

Acceptance (REQUEST_11 section 3), all met and AI-gold-verified (77859, 90203,
xiao all PASS): #1 Table 5 + Table 3 arms; #2 Tables 8/9/10 F/p/eta2p/CI/BF01 +
r/n/CI two-arm; #3 sign-correct CIs + packed cells split; #4 default call
byte-identical + PROSECCO unchanged.

Verification: 25 flatten tests (16 new: packed-arm split, value-group splitter,
single-arm non-firing guard, real-PDF Table 3) all pass serially; full library
suite green under -n10 (1815 passed, 0 failed) and serially; before/after corpus
diff confirms canary structural renders byte-identical and PROSECCO's 6 stat rows
untouched. Value-exact real-PDF tests skip under xdist (Camelot is
non-deterministic under parallel load; they run in the serial canonical QA).

fields.effect_type (REQUEST_11 section 2.4, explicitly "not a blocker") is
deferred: emitting it would change PROSECCO's field set, conflicting with
acceptance #4; offered as an opt-in in the reply.

Two source-data caveats surfaced (neither a flatten defect, both documented in
the reply): collabra.90203 Table 10 "Joint/No-explicit" r reads .59 in the PDF
text layer (pdftotext and Camelot agree) vs .63 in the AI-visual gold (text-layer
corruption, undetectable without OCR); and the collabra.77859 Attractive/Affect
table is labelled "Table 3" by docpluck and the consumer but "Table 2" by the
gold (pre-existing caption-number binding; values all correct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists
recent_commits.body:
code_check used paper$paper_id and a wrong lang/language column name
when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push
recent_commits.body:
v2.4.94 ship lesson: pushing main/tags runs the FULL 5-paper canary render audit
(~5-10 min) before transfer. Killing/timeout-wrapping the push aborts the hook so
nothing lands; `git push | tail` masks it as exit 0. Verify success via git
ls-remote, not exit code; gh auth setup-git for the credential helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94)
recent_commits.body:
ESCImate REQUEST_10. Two tiers, both general (keyed on structural signatures),
zero baseline regressions.

Tier-1 (flatten quality, CHANGELOG v2.4.93):
- combined est_ci columns ("Risk diff. (95% CI)") parse estimate + interval
- dash-sign CI disambiguation via monotonicity + estimate-in-interval invariants
- parallel ITT/PP group rows (one FlattenedRow per row x arm)

Tier-2 (table capture, v2.4.94, TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0):
- _augment_lattice_with_stream_rows: recover rows a lattice extraction
  vertically truncated, by appending the same-page same-col stream rows that
  fall below the lattice bbox (gated: equal-col-count + bbox overlap + extends-below)
- numeric/parenthetical continuation merge in _merge_continuation_rows rejoins
  stream's stacked value/parenthetical cells

Root cause (corrected after grounding in raw per-flavor Camelot output): not
orphaned-label synthesis — stream captured every row but lost headers + split
cells; lattice had headers but only ruled-box rows; page-selection discarded
the fuller stream table. See LESSONS L-009.

PROSECCO Table 2 (10.1371/journal.pmed.1004323) now flattens R1-R6 sign-correct.
Verification: full suite 2051 passed, 26/26 render verifier, service 167 passed,
9 new unit tests; zero regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'main' and 'dev' branches. On the 'dev' branch, the confirmation email and review page were localized to follow the review language, as stated in the commit message "fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)". The 'dev' branch also had a fix for deployment, specifically enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table, as per the commit "fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)". Additionally, the 'dev' branch was updated by merging the 'origin/main' into 'sync/reconcile-v1.8.0' and merging pull request #242 from 'Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0'. The 'main' branch was updated by merging pull request #245 from 'Davidvandijcke/dev'.

recent_commits.body: Deploy: i18n email/review-link fixes + schema_migrations RLS
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Release v1.8.0 — multilingual support
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Deploy: i18n email/review-link fixes + schema_migrations RLS
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Release v1.8.0 — multilingual support
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists
recent_commits.body:
code_check used paper$paper_id and a wrong lang/language column name
when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push
recent_commits.body:
v2.4.94 ship lesson: pushing main/tags runs the FULL 5-paper canary render audit
(~5-10 min) before transfer. Killing/timeout-wrapping the push aborts the hook so
nothing lands; `git push | tail` masks it as exit 0. Verify success via git
ls-remote, not exit code; gh auth setup-git for the credential helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94)
recent_commits.body:
ESCImate REQUEST_10. Two tiers, both general (keyed on structural signatures),
zero baseline regressions.

Tier-1 (flatten quality, CHANGELOG v2.4.93):
- combined est_ci columns ("Risk diff. (95% CI)") parse estimate + interval
- dash-sign CI disambiguation via monotonicity + estimate-in-interval invariants
- parallel ITT/PP group rows (one FlattenedRow per row x arm)

Tier-2 (table capture, v2.4.94, TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0):
- _augment_lattice_with_stream_rows: recover rows a lattice extraction
  vertically truncated, by appending the same-page same-col stream rows that
  fall below the lattice bbox (gated: equal-col-count + bbox overlap + extends-below)
- numeric/parenthetical continuation merge in _merge_continuation_rows rejoins
  stream's stacked value/parenthetical cells

Root cause (corrected after grounding in raw per-flavor Camelot output): not
orphaned-label synthesis — stream captured every row but lost headers + split
cells; lattice had headers but only ruled-box rows; page-selection discarded
the fuller stream table. See LESSONS L-009.

PROSECCO Table 2 (10.1371/journal.pmed.1004323) now flattens R1-R6 sign-correct.
Verification: full suite 2051 passed, 26/26 render verifier, service 167 passed,
9 new unit tests; zero regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

Both of these changes were made by Lakens on the same day, June 19, 2026. 
The `fix/code-check-paperlist` and `papers_databases` branches were the two branches that were worked on in these commits.

The commits were focused on resolving a specific issue and adding new functionality to load paper databases.

recent_commits.message: Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists
recent_commits.body:
code_check used paper$paper_id and a wrong lang/language column name
when building summary_table, which only worked for a single paper and
crashed with "differing number of rows" on a paperlist. repo_check's
file table never carried paper_id, so code_check had no way to
aggregate per paper. Also ensure parse_error/parse_error_msg always
exist with consistent types regardless of file language.
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers
recent_commits.author: Lakens

recent_commits.message: docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push
recent_commits.body:
v2.4.94 ship lesson: pushing main/tags runs the FULL 5-paper canary render audit
(~5-10 min) before transfer. Killing/timeout-wrapping the push aborts the hook so
nothing lands; `git push | tail` masks it as exit 0. Verify success via git
ls-remote, not exit code; gh auth setup-git for the credential helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94)
recent_commits.body:
ESCImate REQUEST_10. Two tiers, both general (keyed on structural signatures),
zero baseline regressions.

Tier-1 (flatten quality, CHANGELOG v2.4.93):
- combined est_ci columns ("Risk diff. (95% CI)") parse estimate + interval
- dash-sign CI disambiguation via monotonicity + estimate-in-interval invariants
- parallel ITT/PP group rows (one FlattenedRow per row x arm)

Tier-2 (table capture, v2.4.94, TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0):
- _augment_lattice_with_stream_rows: recover rows a lattice extraction
  vertically truncated, by appending the same-page same-col stream rows that
  fall below the lattice bbox (gated: equal-col-count + bbox overlap + extends-below)
- numeric/parenthetical continuation merge in _merge_continuation_rows rejoins
  stream's stacked value/parenthetical cells

Root cause (corrected after grounding in raw per-flavor Camelot output): not
orphaned-label synthesis — stream captured every row but lost headers + split
cells; lattice had headers but only ruled-box rows; page-selection discarded
the fuller stream table. See LESSONS L-009.

PROSECCO Table 2 (10.1371/journal.pmed.1004323) now flattens R1-R6 sign-correct.
Verification: full suite 2051 passed, 26/26 render verifier, service 167 passed,
9 new unit tests; zero regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

narrative: Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project, significant updates were made to the 'dev' branch, including localization of the confirmation email and review page, as well as a crucial fix for deployment. Meanwhile, the **JamieCummins/regcheck** project focused on enhancing the staging branch, with improvements to the qwen feature, logging functionality, and efforts to resolve an osf bug. Notably, the updates in **regcheck** were pushed for redeployment, demonstrating the project's momentum. These advancements highlight the connections between our projects, as both **coarse** and **regcheck** prioritize user experience and reliability. As our community continues to drive innovation, we celebrate the tireless efforts of our contributors and look forward to the impact of these updates.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'main' and 'dev' branches. On the 'dev' branch, the confirmation email was localized and the review page now follows the review language, as stated in the commit message "fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)". The 'dev' branch also had a fix for deployment, specifically enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table, as per the commit "fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)". A merge was performed on the 'main' branch, incorporating changes from the 'dev' branch via pull request #245. Additionally, the 'dev' branch was updated by merging remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0, and it also merged pull request #242 from the 'release/v1.8.0' branch.

recent_commits.body: Deploy: i18n email/review-link fixes + schema_migrations RLS
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).
   Placeholders substituted with a function replacer so a filename with
   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another
   device (no localStorage). The review page now applies the review's
   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

Web-only — no src/coarse change, no PyPI impact. Coverage in
tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body: Release v1.8.0 — multilingual support
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)
recent_commits.body:
Two multilingual fast-follows reported after the v1.8.0 launch:

1. The submit-time "your paper is being reviewed" confirmation email was
   hardcoded English. Localize it to the chosen site_language (the only
   language known at submit time) via web/src/lib/confirmationEmail.ts (12
   locales, English fallback; terminology matches the completion email).

   "$&" can't corrupt output.

2. A review opened from its email link showed English chrome on another

   stored language once it loads, via a non-persisting applyInitialLocale
   on the site-language hook (an explicit user switch still wins; the
   global preference is never overwritten). New coerceSiteLocale helper.

tests/test_web_confirmation_email.py.

recent_commits.message: fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)
recent_commits.body:
Supabase Security Advisor flagged public.schema_migrations as exposed to
PostgREST without row-level security. It is internal migration bookkeeping
written only by the service-role path (apply_migrations.sh / SQL editor),
which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no policies locks it from the
anon/authenticated API while migrations keep working — matching the
project's other internal tables. Added at both creation sites (idempotent),
so re-running either secures an existing project.

recent_commits.message: feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94)
recent_commits.body:
ESCImate REQUEST_10. Two tiers, both general (keyed on structural signatures),
zero baseline regressions.

Tier-1 (flatten quality, CHANGELOG v2.4.93):
- combined est_ci columns ("Risk diff. (95% CI)") parse estimate + interval
- dash-sign CI disambiguation via monotonicity + estimate-in-interval invariants
- parallel ITT/PP group rows (one FlattenedRow per row x arm)

Tier-2 (table capture, v2.4.94, TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.3.0):
- _augment_lattice_with_stream_rows: recover rows a lattice extraction
  vertically truncated, by appending the same-page same-col stream rows that
  fall below the lattice bbox (gated: equal-col-count + bbox overlap + extends-below)
- numeric/parenthetical continuation merge in _merge_continuation_rows rejoins
  stream's stacked value/parenthetical cells

Root cause (corrected after grounding in raw per-flavor Camelot output): not
orphaned-label synthesis — stream captured every row but lost headers + split
cells; lattice had headers but only ruled-box rows; page-selection discarded
the fuller stream table. See LESSONS L-009.

PROSECCO Table 2 (10.1371/journal.pmed.1004323) now flattens R1-R6 sign-correct.
Verification: full suite 2051 passed, 26/26 render verifier, service 167 passed,
9 new unit tests; zero regressions.

recent_commits.body:
Run OPEN/PARTIAL. Cycle 2 shipped v2.4.92 affiliation-heading guard (live).
Cycle 3 caption-follows guard reverted (full-corpus gate caught FP regression).
Remaining queue is architectural (table-extraction bbox decision + RC-1),

the bounded-sample-false-confidence lesson.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal
recent_commits.body:
Cycle 2 — shipped v2.4.92 affiliation-heading guard (separate commit cf420ca).
Cycle 3 — caption-follows guard attempted then REVERTED (no release): the full
48-paper corpus regression diff demoted 4 legitimate headings-before-tables
(xiao, amd_2, sci_rep_1, ieee), so the guard was rejected and the tree restored
to v2.4.92 HEAD.

Two durable process lessons recorded (LEARNINGS.md + _project/lessons.md):
1. A render-pass guard sees the PROMOTER-INPUT text, not the final .md — line
   adjacency differs (captions/tables are spliced later). Instrument the actual
   function input; never infer the trigger from finished output.
2. A bounded sample gives false confidence — always run the guard-live-vs-
   bypassed render-diff over the WHOLE corpus before trusting a heading-
   promotion change (rule 19). The FP pattern lives in the long tail.

Also documents the canary-audit-clobbers-phase_5d_runs trap (cycle 2).
No code change in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)
recent_commits.body:
G5d affiliation-heading guard. When column-interleave serialises a two-column
title block out of order, an affiliation line can land past the H1->first-`##`
masthead zone that `_strip_frontmatter_masthead_block` cleans. On canary
`chandrashekar_2023_mp`, "Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College" is
dropped right after `## Abstract`; the masthead strip can no longer reach it,
and its short Title-Case shape then matched the subsection-heading promoter and
became a hallucinated `### Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College`,
corrupting the Abstract's section structure.

Fix (general, keyed on a STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- affiliation grammar, never
paper identity): `_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings` consults a
new `_looks_like_affiliation_line` and refuses to promote any unit-phrase-head
("Department/School/Faculty/Division/Institute/Centre/Center/Laboratory/College/
University of <Cap>") or institution-terminated ("X University/College/Institute/
Hospital/Polytechnic") line. The line stays body text (a strict improvement over
a fake heading; affiliation text preserved, never dropped). Guard runs before
the chain-promotion bypass so it holds on every path.

Verification:
- 47-gold / 2226-real-heading FP scan: zero legitimate headings rejected.
- Deterministic corpus render-diff (guard-live vs guard-bypassed) over canary +
  12-paper cross-publisher sample: ONLY chandrashekar changed (the one target
  heading); all other papers byte-identical.
- Full suite 2027 passed; 227 render/heading cluster; 23 new affiliation-guard
  tests (real-PDF invariant + affiliation grammar contract).
- AI-verify vs article-finder reading gold: FIX-CORRECT, no new text-loss /
  hallucination.

chandrashekar remains FAIL overall on PRE-EXISTING Table 7-10 header-shell
collapse and two pre-existing table-cell-label heading promotions
("IV2: No-default", "Participation rate") -- both queued as separate cycles;
this release does not touch them.

Local $0 experiment (4 Opus-4.8 reviewers, one per output language;
scored with the production quote verifier + an English-only judge) plus
a ~19-source literature review, showing direct-in-language generation
(Design A) does not degrade review quality and that English-pivot +
translate (Design B) would corrupt verbatim quote blocks. Includes the
write-up, raw data, and a re-runnable quote-fidelity scorer.

Dev-only tooling to decide whether direct-in-language review (design A) is
sufficient or whether the English-pivot design (B/PR-G) is warranted.

  (semantic judge matches issues cross-lingually; English baseline = ground
  truth) + programmatic quote_fidelity; recommend() flags a language for

  (like quality.py/recall.py); passes the pipeline-importer arch guard.

  paid; maintainer-run, not CI).

judge mocked). Full suite 1282 passed; ruff + security clean. Dev-only.

recent_commits.body:
Makes the analysis correct for non-English papers (PR-B1/B2 did output language).

- structure.py: multilingual section-typing keywords (es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh/ja/
  ko/ar) so references are excluded + lit-routing/cross-section fire; English
  keywords first -> English typing byte-identical.
- garble.py: stop flagging legitimate accented Latin/® (no spurious garble on
  Romance papers); context-gated ®->fi regex repairs ligature garble without
  corrupting trademarks; normalize runs unconditionally in extraction.
- textscript.py (new) + extraction/headless/web estimateCost: script-aware token
  estimate (CJK ~1.6 vs Latin ~4 chars/token); ==len//4 for Latin (English
  cost/section counts unchanged).
- quote_verify.py + types.py + section.py: char-n-gram candidate selection for
  spaceless CJK; script-aware quote-length floor (>=8 CJK / >=20 Latin) at both
  the model validator and section.py's low-value-quote drop.

Independent of PR-B2. English byte-stable throughout (regression-guarded).
Built by parallel agents + 2-lens adversarial review; fixes folded in
(garble-survives-gate, dead CJK section floor, Spanish Bibliografía, まとめ/Quellen).

Full suite 1211 passed; ruff clean; security clean.

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225)
recent_commits.body:
Makes localized review output zero-config and fully wired on the web.

- languages.py + languages.ts: catalog (code -> {name, direction}) + resolver;
  Python<->TS parity test guards drift.
- Detection folded into the structure-metadata LLM call (PaperMetadata.language
  -> PaperStructure.paper_language); regional tags coerced (pt-BR->pt,
  zh-Hant-HK->zh-Hant; bare zh stays English).
- review_paper resolves effective output language (explicit > detected paper >
  site > English), passes a directive NAME to agents, attaches LanguageContext
  to Review. Explicit/auto English injects no directive -> byte-identical.
- Worker forwards + persists review_language/paper_language/text_direction/
  paper_language_source; CLI handoff finalize forwards them too.
- Web: LanguagePicker on submit; review page sets dir/lang + math-LTR + a
  detected-language badge; Discuss chat / AI handoff / subscription CLI carry
  the language.

Built by parallel agents on disjoint files; hardened by an adversarial review
(explicit-English byte-identity, regional coercion, handoff finalize gap,
result_json.language render fallback, scoped RTL math CSS).

Tests: test_languages, test_structure, test_pipeline, test_modal_worker,
test_web_i18n_contract (+ parity). Full suite 1115 passed; ruff clean.

repos.summary: The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, where the error message was "improve[d]" as per the commit message. The osf bug and the oss bug were specifically "fix[ed]" in separate commits. The codebase also saw the removal of "groq endpoints" and the json handling was "harden[ed]". Additionally, the storage was modified to be "more efficient" and the oss SDK handler was "patch[ed]". All of these changes were made by Jamie Cummins on the staging branch.

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The commit "docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary)" indicates that a handoff was run, version 2.4.92 was shipped, the caption guard was reverted, and work was paused at an architectural boundary. Another commit on the `main` branch, "docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal", added documentation for cycle 2, which includes the affiliation guard for version 2.4.92, and cycle 3, where the caption-follows feature was reverted. A feature commit "feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)" changed the rendering to never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings, specifically for version 2.4.92.

recent_commits.message: docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal
recent_commits.body:
Cycle 2 — shipped v2.4.92 affiliation-heading guard (separate commit cf420ca).
Cycle 3 — caption-follows guard attempted then REVERTED (no release): the full
48-paper corpus regression diff demoted 4 legitimate headings-before-tables
(xiao, amd_2, sci_rep_1, ieee), so the guard was rejected and the tree restored
to v2.4.92 HEAD.

Two durable process lessons recorded (LEARNINGS.md + _project/lessons.md):
1. A render-pass guard sees the PROMOTER-INPUT text, not the final .md — line
   adjacency differs (captions/tables are spliced later). Instrument the actual
   function input; never infer the trigger from finished output.
2. A bounded sample gives false confidence — always run the guard-live-vs-
   bypassed render-diff over the WHOLE corpus before trusting a heading-
   promotion change (rule 19). The FP pattern lives in the long tail.

Also documents the canary-audit-clobbers-phase_5d_runs trap (cycle 2).
No code change in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)
recent_commits.body:
G5d affiliation-heading guard. When column-interleave serialises a two-column
title block out of order, an affiliation line can land past the H1->first-`##`
masthead zone that `_strip_frontmatter_masthead_block` cleans. On canary
`chandrashekar_2023_mp`, "Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College" is
dropped right after `## Abstract`; the masthead strip can no longer reach it,
and its short Title-Case shape then matched the subsection-heading promoter and
became a hallucinated `### Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College`,
corrupting the Abstract's section structure.

Fix (general, keyed on a STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- affiliation grammar, never
paper identity): `_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings` consults a
new `_looks_like_affiliation_line` and refuses to promote any unit-phrase-head
("Department/School/Faculty/Division/Institute/Centre/Center/Laboratory/College/
University of <Cap>") or institution-terminated ("X University/College/Institute/
Hospital/Polytechnic") line. The line stays body text (a strict improvement over
a fake heading; affiliation text preserved, never dropped). Guard runs before
the chain-promotion bypass so it holds on every path.

Verification:
- 47-gold / 2226-real-heading FP scan: zero legitimate headings rejected.
- Deterministic corpus render-diff (guard-live vs guard-bypassed) over canary +
  12-paper cross-publisher sample: ONLY chandrashekar changed (the one target
  heading); all other papers byte-identical.
- Full suite 2027 passed; 227 render/heading cluster; 23 new affiliation-guard
  tests (real-PDF invariant + affiliation grammar contract).
- AI-verify vs article-finder reading gold: FIX-CORRECT, no new text-loss /
  hallucination.

chandrashekar remains FAIL overall on PRE-EXISTING Table 7-10 header-shell
collapse and two pre-existing table-cell-label heading promotions
("IV2: No-default", "Participation rate") -- both queued as separate cycles;
this release does not touch them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

narrative: This week, our open-source research community saw exciting updates across multiple projects. The **scienceverse/metacheck** and **giladfeldman/escicheck** teams made notable progress, with metacheck's 'main' branch receiving a fix for issue #314 and escicheck's main branch being synchronized with effectcheck version 0.6.3. Meanwhile, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** made significant strides in localization, adding support for multiple languages and refining site-UI wording. Additionally, **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** and **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** saw updates, including a revised validation note with a citation and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These updates demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and improvement, and we celebrate the connections between these projects as they continue to advance open-source research tools.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'dev' branch, as well as several feature branches including 'feat/i18n-setup-compare', 'feat/237-lang-eval', 'feat/nl-flemish', 'feat/i18n-status-review-pages', 'feat/231-site-i18n', 'feat/229-i18n-labels', and 'feat/226-i18n-deenglish'. The 'dev' branch and 'feat/i18n-setup-compare' branch saw the addition of localization for setup and compare pages, which now follow the site language, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language". The 'feat/nl-flemish' and 'dev' branches included Dutch site-UI wording refinements, with the commit message specifying "i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements". The 'dev' branch also saw the localization of status and review pages, with the addition of Opus QA for all 12 catalogs, as described in the commit message "feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs". Additionally, the 'dev' branch and 'feat/231-site-i18n' branch included the implementation of a localized website UI with a manual language switcher, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H)".

recent_commits.body:
Dev-only tooling to decide whether direct-in-language review (design A) is
sufficient or whether the English-pivot design (B/PR-G) is warranted.

- src/coarse/lang_eval.py: compare_languages reuses recall.compute_recall
  (semantic judge matches issues cross-lingually; English baseline = ground
  truth) + programmatic quote_fidelity; recommend() flags a language for
  English-pivot if overlap drops or quote fidelity falls/trails English.
  Pure module — operates on Review objects, does NOT import the pipeline
  (like quality.py/recall.py); passes the pipeline-importer arch guard.
- scripts/lang_eval_run.py: the live driver (runs review_paper per language;
  paid; maintainer-run, not CI).

Tests: tests/test_lang_eval.py (fidelity, recommend heuristic, wiring with the
judge mocked). Full suite 1282 passed; ruff + security clean. Dev-only.

recent_commits.body:
A localized review is no longer target-language feedback under English headers.

- review_labels.py + reviewLabels.ts: 13 structural labels (Date/Domain/...,
  Overall Feedback, Outline, Recommendation, Key revision targets, Status/
  Pending, Detailed Comments, Quote, Feedback) keyed by review_language across
  all 11 non-English locales; English values byte-identical to the old literals.
- synthesis.render_review draws labels from review.language.review_language.
- parseReview.ts made language-aware (anchor regexes built from the catalog via
  escapeRegExp; English path reproduces the original literals exactly);
  ReviewPageClient passes review.review_language.
- Worker completion email localized via email_completion_labels.

English / unset review_language => byte-identical (golden-file guarded). Quotes
never translated. Built by parallel agents + adversarial review (verified
English regex equivalence + en/es/ar round-trip + py<->ts parity, no blockers).

Tests: test_synthesis, test_review_labels, test_web_i18n_contract (parity).
Full suite 1266 passed; ruff + security clean. Deferred: confirmation email
(usually English at submit time); site UI chrome is PR-H.

recent_commits.body:
Makes the analysis correct for non-English papers (PR-B1/B2 did output language).

- structure.py: multilingual section-typing keywords (es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh/ja/
  ko/ar) so references are excluded + lit-routing/cross-section fire; English
  keywords first -> English typing byte-identical.
- garble.py: stop flagging legitimate accented Latin/® (no spurious garble on
  Romance papers); context-gated ®->fi regex repairs ligature garble without
  corrupting trademarks; normalize runs unconditionally in extraction.
- textscript.py (new) + extraction/headless/web estimateCost: script-aware token
  estimate (CJK ~1.6 vs Latin ~4 chars/token); ==len//4 for Latin (English
  cost/section counts unchanged).
- quote_verify.py + types.py + section.py: char-n-gram candidate selection for
  spaceless CJK; script-aware quote-length floor (>=8 CJK / >=20 Latin) at both
  the model validator and section.py's low-value-quote drop.

Independent of PR-B2. English byte-stable throughout (regression-guarded).
Built by parallel agents + 2-lens adversarial review; fixes folded in
(garble-survives-gate, dead CJK section floor, Spanish Bibliografía, まとめ/Quellen).

Full suite 1211 passed; ruff clean; security clean.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225)
recent_commits.body:
Makes localized review output zero-config and fully wired on the web.

- languages.py + languages.ts: catalog (code -> {name, direction}) + resolver;
  Python<->TS parity test guards drift.
- Detection folded into the structure-metadata LLM call (PaperMetadata.language
  -> PaperStructure.paper_language); regional tags coerced (pt-BR->pt,
  zh-Hant-HK->zh-Hant; bare zh stays English).
- review_paper resolves effective output language (explicit > detected paper >
  site > English), passes a directive NAME to agents, attaches LanguageContext
  to Review. Explicit/auto English injects no directive -> byte-identical.
- Worker forwards + persists review_language/paper_language/text_direction/
  paper_language_source; CLI handoff finalize forwards them too.
- Web: LanguagePicker on submit; review page sets dir/lang + math-LTR + a
  detected-language badge; Discuss chat / AI handoff / subscription CLI carry
  the language.

Built by parallel agents on disjoint files; hardened by an adversarial review
(explicit-English byte-identity, regional coercion, handoff finalize gap,
result_json.language render fallback, scoped RTL math CSS).

Tests: test_languages, test_structure, test_pipeline, test_modal_worker,
test_web_i18n_contract (+ parity). Full suite 1115 passed; ruff clean.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

narrative: The open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the **scienceverse/metacheck** and **JamieCummins/regcheck** projects have both made strides in improving their tools, with bug fixes and enhancements to their reporting and storage mechanisms, respectively. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** has shipped version 2.4.92 and made notable changes to its documentation and rendering features. The **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project has made impressive gains in localization, adding features such as a manual language switcher and auto-detection. These developments demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and continuous improvement, with many projects tackling complex issues and pushing the boundaries of what's possible. As these projects continue to evolve, we can expect to see even more exciting innovations and connections between them.

The 'feat(i18n): localize status + review pages' feature was added, which includes localizing the status and review pages, and an Opus QA of all 12 catalogs was performed. The website UI was localized with a manual language switcher, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H)". Review headers and completion email were also localized, with the commit message specifying "feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E)". Additionally, the analysis was "de-English-ified" and auto-detection, web language picker, and RTL render were implemented, with the commit message noting "feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2)". The 'feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker' feature was added to the 'dev' branch, which adds GLM 5.2 to the model picker.

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233)
recent_commits.body:
PR-H localized only the submit page, so the status ("queued"/"reviewing")
and review/report pages reverted to English after submitting from a non-English
page. Both routes now wrap their client tree in SiteLanguageProvider (a *Body
split) and use t(); the choice persists in localStorage so it follows across
navigation.

- Externalized status-page strings + review-page UI chrome (the "Discuss with
  your AI" handoff menu, per-comment Discuss chat, Model/Date/Domain/Time/Cost
  metadata labels, download/share/filter/sidebar controls, paper panel) into the
  catalog. Review CONTENT + verbatim quotes untouched (localized separately by
  review-language). Also localized ModelPicker + OpenRouterLoginButton (PR-H
  missed them). en.ts 139 -> 292 keys, English byte-identical.
- All 11 non-English catalogs re-done by per-language Opus-4.8 agents: translate
  the 153 new keys + audit/fix existing translations. Caught real prior bugs
  (doubled "terminal" in ko/ja handoff; specificity->accuracy in de/fr/es;
  pt handoff/download term collision; effort mislabeled in CJK).
- Poll effects kept mount-only (t omitted from deps) to avoid a redundant
  re-fetch on language toggle.

All 12 catalogs validated (292 string keys, no syntax breakage); 146 t() keys
all resolve; English byte-identical (154 values checked); build-soundness
adversarial review = no blockers. tests/test_web_site_i18n.py asserts 292 keys;
full suite 1271 passed. Web-only; Vercel build is the TS gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232)
recent_commits.body:
The site chrome (submit page + header/footer/banner + pickers + status/error
text) can now render in all 12 locales (en + es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/
ja/ko/ar). Distinct from the review-output language — separate control.

- web/src/lib/i18n.ts: useSiteLanguage hook + React context + t() + 12-catalog
  registry; Messages = Record<keyof typeof en, string> (closed key set);
  default English, manual switch, localStorage-persisted (no browser detect);
  Arabic UI ⇒ document.documentElement.dir = rtl.
- SiteLanguageSwitcher in the header.
- 12 catalogs under web/src/lib/i18n/ (139 keys; en.ts canonical, values
  byte-identical to prior literals).
- page.tsx refactored: PageBody wrapped in the provider, ~136 strings → t();
  LanguagePicker consumes the context.

Built by parallel agents (English extraction/wiring first, then one translator
per locale) + an adversarial build-soundness review (no blockers: all t() keys
exist, exports match imports, no circular import, English byte-equivalent,
SSR/RTL sound). Coverage: tests/test_web_site_i18n.py (key/export/registry
completeness — no local TS build). Web-only; Vercel build is the TS gate.

The first commit "harden json handling" suggests that the JSON handling in the repository was made more robust. Another commit, "docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal", added documentation for cycle 2, which includes the affiliation guard for version 2.4.92, and cycle 3, where the caption-follows feature was reverted. recent_commits.message: docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary)
recent_commits.body:
Run OPEN/PARTIAL. Cycle 2 shipped v2.4.92 affiliation-heading guard (live).
Cycle 3 caption-follows guard reverted (full-corpus gate caught FP regression).
Remaining queue is architectural (table-extraction bbox decision + RC-1),
paused by user direction. Records the canary-audit phase_5d clobber trap and
the bounded-sample-false-confidence lesson.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal
recent_commits.body:
Cycle 2 — shipped v2.4.92 affiliation-heading guard (separate commit cf420ca).
Cycle 3 — caption-follows guard attempted then REVERTED (no release): the full
48-paper corpus regression diff demoted 4 legitimate headings-before-tables
(xiao, amd_2, sci_rep_1, ieee), so the guard was rejected and the tree restored
to v2.4.92 HEAD.

Two durable process lessons recorded (LEARNINGS.md + _project/lessons.md):
1. A render-pass guard sees the PROMOTER-INPUT text, not the final .md — line
   adjacency differs (captions/tables are spliced later). Instrument the actual
   function input; never infer the trigger from finished output.
2. A bounded sample gives false confidence — always run the guard-live-vs-
   bypassed render-diff over the WHOLE corpus before trusting a heading-
   promotion change (rule 19). The FP pattern lives in the long tail.

Also documents the canary-audit-clobbers-phase_5d_runs trap (cycle 2).
No code change in this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)
recent_commits.body:
G5d affiliation-heading guard. When column-interleave serialises a two-column
title block out of order, an affiliation line can land past the H1->first-`##`
masthead zone that `_strip_frontmatter_masthead_block` cleans. On canary
`chandrashekar_2023_mp`, "Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College" is
dropped right after `## Abstract`; the masthead strip can no longer reach it,

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232)
recent_commits.body:
The site chrome (submit page + header/footer/banner + pickers + status/error
text) can now render in all 12 locales (en + es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/
ja/ko/ar). Distinct from the review-output language — separate control.

- web/src/lib/i18n.ts: useSiteLanguage hook + React context + t() + 12-catalog
  registry; Messages = Record<keyof typeof en, string> (closed key set);
  default English, manual switch, localStorage-persisted (no browser detect);
  Arabic UI ⇒ document.documentElement.dir = rtl.
- SiteLanguageSwitcher in the header.
- 12 catalogs under web/src/lib/i18n/ (139 keys; en.ts canonical, values
  byte-identical to prior literals).
- page.tsx refactored: PageBody wrapped in the provider, ~136 strings → t();
  LanguagePicker consumes the context.

Built by parallel agents (English extraction/wiring first, then one translator
per locale) + an adversarial build-soundness review (no blockers: all t() keys
exist, exports match imports, no circular import, English byte-equivalent,
SSR/RTL sound). Coverage: tests/test_web_site_i18n.py (key/export/registry
completeness — no local TS build). Web-only; Vercel build is the TS gate.

recent_commits.body:
Makes the analysis correct for non-English papers (PR-B1/B2 did output language).

- structure.py: multilingual section-typing keywords (es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh/ja/
  ko/ar) so references are excluded + lit-routing/cross-section fire; English
  keywords first -> English typing byte-identical.
- garble.py: stop flagging legitimate accented Latin/® (no spurious garble on
  Romance papers); context-gated ®->fi regex repairs ligature garble without
  corrupting trademarks; normalize runs unconditionally in extraction.
- textscript.py (new) + extraction/headless/web estimateCost: script-aware token
  estimate (CJK ~1.6 vs Latin ~4 chars/token); ==len//4 for Latin (English
  cost/section counts unchanged).
- quote_verify.py + types.py + section.py: char-n-gram candidate selection for
  spaceless CJK; script-aware quote-length floor (>=8 CJK / >=20 Latin) at both
  the model validator and section.py's low-value-quote drop.

Independent of PR-B2. English byte-stable throughout (regression-guarded).
Built by parallel agents + 2-lens adversarial review; fixes folded in
(garble-survives-gate, dead CJK section floor, Spanish Bibliografía, まとめ/Quellen).

Full suite 1211 passed; ruff clean; security clean.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

recent_commits.message: feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225)
recent_commits.body:
Makes localized review output zero-config and fully wired on the web.

- languages.py + languages.ts: catalog (code -> {name, direction}) + resolver;
  Python<->TS parity test guards drift.
- Detection folded into the structure-metadata LLM call (PaperMetadata.language
  -> PaperStructure.paper_language); regional tags coerced (pt-BR->pt,
  zh-Hant-HK->zh-Hant; bare zh stays English).
- review_paper resolves effective output language (explicit > detected paper >
  site > English), passes a directive NAME to agents, attaches LanguageContext
  to Review. Explicit/auto English injects no directive -> byte-identical.
- Worker forwards + persists review_language/paper_language/text_direction/
  paper_language_source; CLI handoff finalize forwards them too.
- Web: LanguagePicker on submit; review page sets dir/lang + math-LTR + a
  detected-language badge; Discuss chat / AI handoff / subscription CLI carry
  the language.

Built by parallel agents on disjoint files; hardened by an adversarial review
(explicit-English byte-identity, regional coercion, handoff finalize gap,
result_json.language render fallback, scoped RTL math CSS).

Tests: test_languages, test_structure, test_pipeline, test_modal_worker,
test_web_i18n_contract (+ parity). Full suite 1115 passed; ruff clean.

recent_commits.message: feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)
recent_commits.body:
G5d affiliation-heading guard. When column-interleave serialises a two-column
title block out of order, an affiliation line can land past the H1->first-`##`
masthead zone that `_strip_frontmatter_masthead_block` cleans. On canary
`chandrashekar_2023_mp`, "Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College" is
dropped right after `## Abstract`; the masthead strip can no longer reach it,
and its short Title-Case shape then matched the subsection-heading promoter and
became a hallucinated `### Department of Philosophy, Lake Forest College`,
corrupting the Abstract's section structure.

Fix (general, keyed on a STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- affiliation grammar, never
paper identity): `_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings` consults a
new `_looks_like_affiliation_line` and refuses to promote any unit-phrase-head
("Department/School/Faculty/Division/Institute/Centre/Center/Laboratory/College/
University of <Cap>") or institution-terminated ("X University/College/Institute/
Hospital/Polytechnic") line. The line stays body text (a strict improvement over
a fake heading; affiliation text preserved, never dropped). Guard runs before
the chain-promotion bypass so it holds on every path.

Verification:
- 47-gold / 2226-real-heading FP scan: zero legitimate headings rejected.
- Deterministic corpus render-diff (guard-live vs guard-bypassed) over canary +
  12-paper cross-publisher sample: ONLY chandrashekar changed (the one target
  heading); all other papers byte-identical.
- Full suite 2027 passed; 227 render/heading cluster; 23 new affiliation-guard
  tests (real-PDF invariant + affiliation grammar contract).
- AI-verify vs article-finder reading gold: FIX-CORRECT, no new text-loss /
  hallucination.
