collapse and two pre-existing table-cell-label heading promotions
("IV2: No-default", "Participation rate") -- both queued as separate cycles;

recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.body:
Promote glued JESP/Elsevier single-column subsection headings ("Overview",
"Practice instructions", "Self-control assessment") that sit on their own line
with NO blank padding on either side, without re-opening the two-column G5d
hallucinated-heading trap.

_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings now admits a no-blank-before
candidate only when (a) the document is single-column — _raw_text_is_single_column,
the fraction of raw-pdftotext lines wider than 65 chars >= 0.25, a structural
typographic invariant computed from the RAW text before the render pipeline joins
column-wrapped lines and destroys the width signal (corpus separation: two-column
0.06-0.24, single-column 0.28-0.58) — AND (b) the preceding line is a genuine
clean paragraph boundary (sentence terminator or markdown heading; stricter than
_prev_paragraph_is_sentence_terminated to reject chen_2021_jesp scale-item wraps).
A fragment guard (_is_single_col_relaxation_fragment) rejects bracket furniture,
leading-preposition sentence-wraps, dangling-connector tails, and abbreviation-
glossary / clause lists (internal ";" or trailing ",").

Validation: ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011 +3 genuine headings (AI-verified vs the
article-finder reading gold: new_headings_are_real=true, zero hallucination);
jmf_1 +11, ar_apa_010 +9, demography_1 +4, bjps_1 +3, chen_2021_jesp +3;
two-column papers (ip_feldman_2025_pspb byte-identical, chan_feldman, chandrashekar)
+0; plos_med_1 net +0 (one abbreviation-glossary false promotion caught by the
fragment guard). 26-paper baseline 26/26; full suite 1748 passed. New regression
tests/test_single_column_subsection_promote_real_pdf.py (23 cases).

The canary corpus still FAILs on pre-existing table row/column loss and RC-1
column-interleave (separate root causes, queued) — incremental heading-fidelity
improvement, not a corpus-clean claim.

Provenance: render.py combines work from a concurrent docpluck-iterate stream
(the chen_2021_jesp _prev_is_clean_boundary guard + _LEADING_FRAGMENT_PREPS) with
this session (the _raw_text_is_single_column single-column gate + the abbreviation-
glossary ";"/"," fragment guard). One coherent feature; verified together.

recent_commits.body:
Makes the analysis correct for non-English papers (PR-B1/B2 did output language).

- structure.py: multilingual section-typing keywords (es/fr/de/nl/pt/it/zh/ja/
  ko/ar) so references are excluded + lit-routing/cross-section fire; English
  keywords first -> English typing byte-identical.
- garble.py: stop flagging legitimate accented Latin/® (no spurious garble on
  Romance papers); context-gated ®->fi regex repairs ligature garble without
  corrupting trademarks; normalize runs unconditionally in extraction.
- textscript.py (new) + extraction/headless/web estimateCost: script-aware token
  estimate (CJK ~1.6 vs Latin ~4 chars/token); ==len//4 for Latin (English
  cost/section counts unchanged).
- quote_verify.py + types.py + section.py: char-n-gram candidate selection for
  spaceless CJK; script-aware quote-length floor (>=8 CJK / >=20 Latin) at both
  the model validator and section.py's low-value-quote drop.

Independent of PR-B2. English byte-stable throughout (regression-guarded).
Built by parallel agents + 2-lens adversarial review; fixes folded in
(garble-survives-gate, dead CJK section floor, Spanish Bibliografía, まとめ/Quellen).

  the language.

Built by parallel agents on disjoint files; hardened by an adversarial review
(explicit-English byte-identity, regional coercion, handoff finalize gap,
result_json.language render fallback, scoped RTL math CSS).

recent_commits.body:
The review now comes out in a chosen language, end-to-end, on the CLI and
subscription-handoff paths.

- prompts.language_directive() (appended via feedback_system_prompt) tells the
  six feedback agents to write prose in the target language, keep verbatim
  quotes in the paper's SOURCE language, leave LaTeX alone, and apply the
  English humanizer/tone rules' intent idiomatically (not the literal word list).
- `language` threads review_paper() -> review_stages -> overview/completeness/
  section/cross-section/verify/editorial, mirroring author_notes.
- --language on `coarse review`, `coarse-review`, `coarse.headless_review`;
  review_language default in CoarseConfig. Passed as a language NAME ("French").
- Works for litellm and subscription backends. Unset = byte-identical English.

Tests: test_prompts (directive + byte-identical guard), test_overview (agent
forwards language into the system prompt), test_config, plus updated
agent/pipeline/CLI fakes.

recent_commits.body:
First, behavior-neutral step of the multilingual rollout
(docs/MULTILINGUAL_PLAN.md): add the language contract end-to-end
(DB -> web routes -> worker) so later PRs can detect paper language and
localize the review. No behavior change -- every field is nullable/optional
and unset = today's output.

- reviews: + site_language, review_language, paper_language,
  paper_language_source, text_direction (ltr/rtl check) + indexes; idempotent
  deploy/migrate_i18n_contract.sql. Reuses result_json (no review_json);
  taxonomy already existed.
- LanguageContext (src/coarse/types.py) + TS mirror; review_language defaults
  empty = "follow detected paper language" (resolved in the worker later).
- submit + mcp-finalize routes accept/normalize/validate language via a shared
  BCP-47-aware web/src/lib/language.ts (preserves zh-Hant/pt-BR casing); submit
  forwards review_language/site_language to the Modal worker (ReviewRequest).
- review/[id] SELECT unions the new columns and keeps result_json.
- analysis_language intentionally omitted (no consumer under the MVP design).

Tests: tests/test_types.py (LanguageContext), tests/test_web_i18n_contract.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

recent_commits.body:
Promote glued JESP/Elsevier single-column subsection headings ("Overview",
"Practice instructions", "Self-control assessment") that sit on their own line
with NO blank padding on either side, without re-opening the two-column G5d
hallucinated-heading trap.

_promote_isolated_titlecase_subsection_headings now admits a no-blank-before
candidate only when (a) the document is single-column — _raw_text_is_single_column,
the fraction of raw-pdftotext lines wider than 65 chars >= 0.25, a structural
typographic invariant computed from the RAW text before the render pipeline joins
column-wrapped lines and destroys the width signal (corpus separation: two-column
0.06-0.24, single-column 0.28-0.58) — AND (b) the preceding line is a genuine
clean paragraph boundary (sentence terminator or markdown heading; stricter than
_prev_paragraph_is_sentence_terminated to reject chen_2021_jesp scale-item wraps).
A fragment guard (_is_single_col_relaxation_fragment) rejects bracket furniture,
leading-preposition sentence-wraps, dangling-connector tails, and abbreviation-
glossary / clause lists (internal ";" or trailing ",").

Validation: ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011 +3 genuine headings (AI-verified vs the
article-finder reading gold: new_headings_are_real=true, zero hallucination);
jmf_1 +11, ar_apa_010 +9, demography_1 +4, bjps_1 +3, chen_2021_jesp +3;
two-column papers (ip_feldman_2025_pspb byte-identical, chan_feldman, chandrashekar)
+0; plos_med_1 net +0 (one abbreviation-glossary false promotion caught by the
fragment guard). 26-paper baseline 26/26; full suite 1748 passed. New regression
tests/test_single_column_subsection_promote_real_pdf.py (23 cases).

The canary corpus still FAILs on pre-existing table row/column loss and RC-1
column-interleave (separate root causes, queued) — incremental heading-fidelity
improvement, not a corpus-clean claim.

Provenance: render.py combines work from a concurrent docpluck-iterate stream
(the chen_2021_jesp _prev_is_clean_boundary guard + _LEADING_FRAGMENT_PREPS) with
this session (the _raw_text_is_single_column single-column gate + the abbreviation-
glossary ";"/"," fragment guard). One coherent feature; verified together.

narrative: Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, **JamieCummins/regcheck** has made strides in production preparation, adding HTML and OSF support, and introducing a "light mode" to the frontend. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/escicheck** has been synced with the latest versions of effectcheck, bringing the repository up to date with the latest changes. Additionally, **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** has revised its validation note and added a citation, while **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** has expanded its capabilities with the addition of a Chrome Webstore link and a list of detectable statements and data repositories. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across these projects.

repos.summary: The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The work began with "prod preparation: phase 1 + 2" as stated in the commit message, indicating initial preparation for production. The repository then saw the addition of HTML and OSF support, as well as "harden osf handling", which suggests enhanced handling of OSF. Additionally, the commit "readd reasoning effort for oss" was made, and the "Update privacy.html" commit modified the privacy.html file. The frontend also received updates, including the introduction of "light mode" and fixes for "scroll and gpt-oss bugs", along with the addition of "claude functionality".

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where Gilad Feldman synced the repository from 'escicheckapp' at various commit hashes, specifically 'e060e98' for effectcheck v0.6.2, '5093197' for effectcheck v0.6.1, and '1ebe05f' for effectcheck v0.5.18. A temporary branch 'sync/20260525-150441-b801333' was also used by github-actions[bot] to sync from 'escicheckapp' at commit 'b801333' for effectcheck v0.6.2. The commit messages indicate that these syncs were done to update the repository to match the respective versions of effectcheck. The sync operations brought the 'main' branch up to date with the latest changes from 'escicheckapp', specifically effectcheck versions v0.5.18, v0.6.1, and v0.6.2.

narrative: Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. In the **scienceverse/metacheck** project, issue #313 was successfully addressed through multiple commits from Daniel Lakens, demonstrating the team's dedication to resolving key issues. Meanwhile, the **giladfeldman/docpluck** project underwent substantial updates, including documentation improvements, code refactoring, and the addition of new features such as optional extraction guardrails and logging. Notably, the **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project made strides in internationalization, adding multilingual support and localized review output, which may have implications for other projects like **JamieCummins/regcheck**, where OSF handling was hardened and support for HTML and OSF was added. Overall, these updates showcase the community's commitment to collaboration, improvement, and innovation.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'main' branch, where documentation updates were made, including shipping a handoff and cycle journal for version 2.4.91, as well as adding a root README landing page and syncing normalization and benchmark claims with the current code. The codebase was also refactored to surface fallback exception classes in method metadata and to remove an orphaned duplicate band re-extractor in the 'extract_columns' module. New features were added, including optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout, fallback telemetry counters, and optional logging. Additionally, a continuous integration guard was added to ensure docs-code consistency, and an investigation was queued for items 3 and 8 in the todo list. The rotating pool was also updated to onboard new members, including efendic, maier, and xiao, with rotation now active.

recent_commits.message: refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor
recent_commits.body:
A concurrent docpluck-iterate session and this one independently implemented
RC-1 Step 2. The other session's `extract_page_text_banded` (+ splice/extract.py
wiring + DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED flag) is the canonical wired
implementation, shipped as v2.4.90. This session's parallel
`extract_page_text_bands` (+ `_min_crossing_gutter` / `_row_is_two_column` /
`_segment_into_bands` / `_GUTTER_STRIP_HALF`) was uncommitted at the time and got
swept into the v2.4.90 release commit (1325d14) by the other session's `git add`
as orphaned, never-called dead code.

Removed the entire orphaned block (~190 lines). Inert: distinct names (no
shadowing), referenced only within its own block, ZERO callers (the splice calls
`extract_page_text_banded`). Behaviour-identical removal.

No version bump / re-tag / redeploy: pure no-op cleanup. The active path is
byte-identical; the inert duplicate remains in the already-tagged v2.4.90 / prod
(harmless, uncalled) and rides out with the next tagged release.

SKIP_CANARY=1 justified (per memory feedback_canary_gate_nondeterministic): the
removed functions have ZERO callers, so every rendered output is byte-identical
by construction -- a stronger guarantee than a render-diff. No render.py change.
Verified: AST + import OK; 69 column-path tests + full suite (1978 passed, 27
skipped, 1 xfailed) green before the overnight Dropbox re-sync reverted the file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

The 'privacy.html' file was updated with new content, and frontend updates were made, including the addition of a light mode. Additionally, bugs related to scrolling and GPT-OSS were fixed, and functionality for Claude was added. The repository also saw fixes for bugs, specifically the scroll and GPT-OSS bugs, which were addressed in a separate commit.

Work was done on the 'dev', 'feat/221-i18n-localized-review', and 'feat/219-i18n-schema-contract' branches. On the 'dev' branch, OpenRouter Fusion was added as a displayed model option and its dynamic-pricing estimates were fixed, as stated in the commit message "feat: add OpenRouter Fusion as a displayed model option + fix its dynamic-pricing estimates". The 'feat/221-i18n-localized-review' branch saw the addition of localized review output via the --language option, with the commit message noting "feat(i18n): localized review output via --language". The 'dev' and 'feat/219-i18n-schema-contract' branches both had a multilingual schema and API contract added, with the commit message specifying "feat(i18n): multilingual schema + API contract". Additionally, a CodeQL analysis workflow configuration was added to the 'dev' branch.

recent_commits.author: David Van Dijcke

recent_commits.body:
The review now comes out in a chosen language, end-to-end, on the CLI and
subscription-handoff paths.

- prompts.language_directive() (appended via feedback_system_prompt) tells the
  six feedback agents to write prose in the target language, keep verbatim
  quotes in the paper's SOURCE language, leave LaTeX alone, and apply the
  English humanizer/tone rules' intent idiomatically (not the literal word list).
- `language` threads review_paper() -> review_stages -> overview/completeness/
  section/cross-section/verify/editorial, mirroring author_notes.
- --language on `coarse review`, `coarse-review`, `coarse.headless_review`;
  review_language default in CoarseConfig. Passed as a language NAME ("French").
- Works for litellm and subscription backends. Unset = byte-identical English.

Tests: test_prompts (directive + byte-identical guard), test_overview (agent
forwards language into the system prompt), test_config, plus updated
agent/pipeline/CLI fakes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Davidvandijcke

recent_commits.body:
First, behavior-neutral step of the multilingual rollout
(docs/MULTILINGUAL_PLAN.md): add the language contract end-to-end
(DB -> web routes -> worker) so later PRs can detect paper language and
localize the review. No behavior change -- every field is nullable/optional
and unset = today's output.

- reviews: + site_language, review_language, paper_language,
  paper_language_source, text_direction (ltr/rtl check) + indexes; idempotent
  deploy/migrate_i18n_contract.sql. Reuses result_json (no review_json);
  taxonomy already existed.
- LanguageContext (src/coarse/types.py) + TS mirror; review_language defaults
  empty = "follow detected paper language" (resolved in the worker later).
- submit + mcp-finalize routes accept/normalize/validate language via a shared
  BCP-47-aware web/src/lib/language.ts (preserves zh-Hant/pt-BR casing); submit
  forwards review_language/site_language to the Modal worker (ReviewRequest).
- review/[id] SELECT unions the new columns and keeps result_json.
- analysis_language intentionally omitted (no consumer under the MVP design).

Tests: tests/test_types.py (LanguageContext), tests/test_web_i18n_contract.py.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

narrative: Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. The `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has added several new features, including optional extraction guardrails and fallback telemetry counters, while also refining its documentation and continuous integration checks. Meanwhile, the `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has enhanced its code analysis capabilities with the addition of a CodeQL analysis workflow configuration. The `JamieCummins/regcheck` project has made notable updates, including the addition of HTML and OSF support, a new light mode, and bug fixes. Additionally, the `giladfeldman/escicheck` and `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` projects have seen updates, with the former syncing with `escicheckapp` to incorporate new effectcheck versions and the latter revising its validation note and adding a citation. These connections and advancements demonstrate the collaborative spirit and dedication to improvement within our community.

recent_commits.message: refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor
recent_commits.body:
A concurrent docpluck-iterate session and this one independently implemented
RC-1 Step 2. The other session's `extract_page_text_banded` (+ splice/extract.py
wiring + DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED flag) is the canonical wired
implementation, shipped as v2.4.90. This session's parallel
`extract_page_text_bands` (+ `_min_crossing_gutter` / `_row_is_two_column` /
`_segment_into_bands` / `_GUTTER_STRIP_HALF`) was uncommitted at the time and got
swept into the v2.4.90 release commit (1325d14) by the other session's `git add`
as orphaned, never-called dead code.

Removed the entire orphaned block (~190 lines). Inert: distinct names (no
shadowing), referenced only within its own block, ZERO callers (the splice calls
`extract_page_text_banded`). Behaviour-identical removal.

No version bump / re-tag / redeploy: pure no-op cleanup. The active path is
byte-identical; the inert duplicate remains in the already-tagged v2.4.90 / prod
(harmless, uncalled) and rides out with the next tagged release.

SKIP_CANARY=1 justified (per memory feedback_canary_gate_nondeterministic): the
removed functions have ZERO callers, so every rendered output is byte-identical
by construction -- a stronger guarantee than a render-diff. No render.py change.
Verified: AST + import OK; 69 column-path tests + full suite (1978 passed, 27
skipped, 1 xfailed) green before the overnight Dropbox re-sync reverted the file.

The latest version, 2.4.90, introduces a significant new feature: "per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction", which demonstrates the project's ongoing commitment to innovation. By connecting projects like **giladfeldman/docpluck** with other open-source research tools, we can unlock new possibilities for scientific discovery and progress.

recent_commits.message: feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark)
recent_commits.body:
The dominant defect on two-column APA papers: pdftotext serializes a page's
columns interleaved, scrambling Method/Results/Discussion order and splitting
paragraphs. The Step-1 whole-page corrector (v2.4.82) cannot reach these pages
-- its bilateral y-row gate and full-height gutter strip both reject any page
carrying a full-width band (table row / banner / wide title) crossing the column
centre, which is exactly the failing pages (TRIAGE 2026-06-15: GENERAL=1 was
byte-near-identical on these papers).

Step 2 (extract_page_text_banded, docpluck/extract_columns.py) segments a
flagged page into horizontal y-BANDS: a band whose central gutter strip is
glyph-free is two-column prose -> re-extracted left-then-right; a full-width band
(table / banner / title) is kept as-is. Bands reassemble top-to-bottom at
glyph-free cut lines (vertically-overlapping bands merge to full-width so a tall
title glyph is never bisected). Applied ONLY as a fallback inside
splice_column_corrected_pages when the whole-page path returns "", under the SAME
unconditional word-preservation guard -- a band crop that drops/fabricates a word
is rejected and the page kept as-is, so the flag can only ADD a pure reorder,
never ship corruption.

Gated behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED (default OFF, ship-dark): flag OFF is
byte-identical to v2.4.89. No NORMALIZATION_VERSION change (default path
unchanged; the flag acts upstream of normalize).

Validation (2026-06-15): corpus word-preservation scan (71 flagged pages, 4
papers) -- every accepted page is a pure reorder; the 6 residual band-cut clips
are all guard-rejected (no corruption). Production smoke flag-ON corrects ~44
pages across 5 papers, all word-safe; flag-OFF byte-identical. AI-verify vs
article-finder reading golds (Sonnet subagents) on chan_feldman_2025_cogemo +
chandrashekar_2023_mp: both ON_BETTER -- Procedure/Manipulations ordering and the
Power-analysis paragraph split fixed (chan); `## Results` heading restored and
column-token orphans suppressed (chandrashekar); 0 text-loss/hallucination/
regression vs flag OFF. Touched-path suite 302 passed; 26-paper baseline 26/26
(flag OFF).

New regression tests/test_rc1_banded_column_real_pdf.py (synthetic-layout
geometry units + real-PDF word-preservation + ship-dark-default). Remaining
Step-2 refinements (band-cut clips, per-row both-sides under-detection,
title+sidebar pages) documented in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-rc1-region-
aware-column-architecture.md for the eventual default-flip.

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel
recent_commits.body:
The no-CI residual W0g cannot reach: a body-prose coefficient with only a t/p
value (b = -.022, t(87) = .17) where pdftotext drops the U+2212 glyph entirely
on tight-kerned PDFs that draw the minus in a dedicated symbol font. The dropped
minus survives in the layout channel (pdfplumber) as an unmapped (cid:N) glyph
in the `<stat> = <minus><coef>` operator slot; recover_dropped_minus_via_layout
(W0h) re-inserts it.

- normalize.py: W0h + _layout_negative_coefficients (y-overlap line clustering,
  =-anchored coef slot, flips only layout-proven negatives, count-limited).
  NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.34 -> 1.9.35.
- Threaded a DEDICATED `dropped_minus_layout` param (render -> extract_sections
  -> normalize_text) so F0 stays off in the text-channel-only section path.
- ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011: recovers b = -.022 / -.88 / -.428; leaves +.48.
  Surgical blast radius (only ar_apa flips; 4 other canary papers byte-identical).

Documented limitation: ar_apa b = -.245 is painted pixels (absent from pdftotext
AND pdfplumber chars/lines/rects/curves AND pdfminer raw layer) -> OCR-only, left
untouched rather than guessed. See TODO.md R5 Path 1.

Tests: tests/test_dropped_minus_layout_recovery_real_pdf.py (4 unit + 1 real-PDF).
Full suite 1968 passed / 27 skipped / 1 xfailed. AI-verify: all 5 ar_apa beta
signs correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

recent_commits.message: test(canary): assert audit input PDF byte-equal to pinned feed (R-0003)
recent_commits.body:
Cross-project lesson transfer (CitationGuard => docpluck): the verification
substrate must be byte-equal to the production input feed before scoring. A
CitationGuard run once scored its tool output against raw PyMuPDF for a whole
run, producing a misleading score.

docpluck already scores render-vs-AI-gold (never a deterministic extractor --
CLAUDE.md hard rule), so the residual gap that lesson maps to here is INPUT
provenance: a render of a drifted PDF scored against a gold made from the
original is the same "wrong substrate" bug class. If the cached PDF for a DOI
ever drifts (re-download, re-encode, a stray Dropbox conflicted-copy), the
canary would silently score a render of a different input than the gold.

Gate: 10/10 new tests pass; full library suite 1710 passed / 0 failed (no

The Sonnet canary itself is not runnable in this env (ai-gold get --view reading
errors + claude -p nesting hang); provenance is a pre-render guard that does not

recent_commits.message: feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark)
recent_commits.body:
The dominant defect on two-column APA papers: pdftotext serializes a page's
columns interleaved, scrambling Method/Results/Discussion order and splitting
paragraphs. The Step-1 whole-page corrector (v2.4.82) cannot reach these pages
-- its bilateral y-row gate and full-height gutter strip both reject any page
carrying a full-width band (table row / banner / wide title) crossing the column
centre, which is exactly the failing pages (TRIAGE 2026-06-15: GENERAL=1 was
byte-near-identical on these papers).

Step 2 (extract_page_text_banded, docpluck/extract_columns.py) segments a
flagged page into horizontal y-BANDS: a band whose central gutter strip is
glyph-free is two-column prose -> re-extracted left-then-right; a full-width band
(table / banner / title) is kept as-is. Bands reassemble top-to-bottom at
glyph-free cut lines (vertically-overlapping bands merge to full-width so a tall
title glyph is never bisected). Applied ONLY as a fallback inside
splice_column_corrected_pages when the whole-page path returns "", under the SAME
unconditional word-preservation guard -- a band crop that drops/fabricates a word
is rejected and the page kept as-is, so the flag can only ADD a pure reorder,
never ship corruption.

Gated behind DOCPLUCK_COLUMN_CORRECT_BANDED (default OFF, ship-dark): flag OFF is
byte-identical to v2.4.89. No NORMALIZATION_VERSION change (default path
unchanged; the flag acts upstream of normalize).

Validation (2026-06-15): corpus word-preservation scan (71 flagged pages, 4
papers) -- every accepted page is a pure reorder; the 6 residual band-cut clips
are all guard-rejected (no corruption). Production smoke flag-ON corrects ~44
pages across 5 papers, all word-safe; flag-OFF byte-identical. AI-verify vs
article-finder reading golds (Sonnet subagents) on chan_feldman_2025_cogemo +
chandrashekar_2023_mp: both ON_BETTER -- Procedure/Manipulations ordering and the
Power-analysis paragraph split fixed (chan); `## Results` heading restored and
column-token orphans suppressed (chandrashekar); 0 text-loss/hallucination/
regression vs flag OFF. Touched-path suite 302 passed; 26-paper baseline 26/26
(flag OFF).

New regression tests/test_rc1_banded_column_real_pdf.py (synthetic-layout
geometry units + real-PDF word-preservation + ship-dark-default). Remaining
Step-2 refinements (band-cut clips, per-row both-sides under-detection,
title+sidebar pages) documented in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-rc1-region-
aware-column-architecture.md for the eventual default-flip.

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.89): B7 W0h -- recover dropped-minus coefficients from the layout channel
recent_commits.body:
The no-CI residual W0g cannot reach: a body-prose coefficient with only a t/p
value (b = -.022, t(87) = .17) where pdftotext drops the U+2212 glyph entirely
on tight-kerned PDFs that draw the minus in a dedicated symbol font. The dropped
minus survives in the layout channel (pdfplumber) as an unmapped (cid:N) glyph
in the `<stat> = <minus><coef>` operator slot; recover_dropped_minus_via_layout
(W0h) re-inserts it.

- normalize.py: W0h + _layout_negative_coefficients (y-overlap line clustering,
  =-anchored coef slot, flips only layout-proven negatives, count-limited).
  NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.34 -> 1.9.35.
- Threaded a DEDICATED `dropped_minus_layout` param (render -> extract_sections
  -> normalize_text) so F0 stays off in the text-channel-only section path.
- ar_apa_j_jesp_2009_12_011: recovers b = -.022 / -.88 / -.428; leaves +.48.
  Surgical blast radius (only ar_apa flips; 4 other canary papers byte-identical).

Documented limitation: ar_apa b = -.245 is painted pixels (absent from pdftotext
AND pdfplumber chars/lines/rects/curves AND pdfminer raw layer) -> OCR-only, left
untouched rather than guessed. See TODO.md R5 Path 1.

Tests: tests/test_dropped_minus_layout_recovery_real_pdf.py (4 unit + 1 real-PDF).
Full suite 1968 passed / 27 skipped / 1 xfailed. AI-verify: all 5 ar_apa beta
signs correct.

recent_commits.message: chore(canary,env): fix canary render python (py -3) + consolidate Python to global site-packages
recent_commits.body:
Root cause of the pre-push canary false-block ("No module named docpluck",
RENDER FAILED x5) was a Python-env issue, NOT a render regression: all deps
lived in the USER site-packages (AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314), invisible
under `python -s` / PYTHONNOUSERSITE -- the mode the git hook / headless render
subprocess runs in.

- .claude/skills/_project/canary.json: render_command `python` -> `py -3`.
- tools/fix_python_env.ps1: one-time elevated grant of Modify on
  C:\Python314\Lib\site-packages + Scripts; deps reinstalled into the GLOBAL
  site (verified importable under `python -s`). Future pip installs now land in
  the global site automatically -> no recurrence.
- TODO.md: record the v2.4.86/87/88 push + the Python consolidation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

recent_commits.message: fix(v2.4.86/87/88): ScienceArena re-audit — span x-gap spacing, F0 body-from-text-channel, camelot Windows table fix
recent_commits.body:
ScienceArena's public PDF leaderboard showed docpluck below GROBID (sections,
tables) and below GROBID+liteparse (text). Two findings:
  (1) GROBID-beats-docpluck is an ARENA artifact — leaderboard.py::aggregate()
      flat-means over asymmetric task sets (GROBID ran only the synthetic
      subset; on common tasks docpluck >= all). Handed back to ScienceArena via
      HANDOFF_2026-06-13_pdf_arena_global_fairness.md (fix globally at the
      framework layer, all players + all arenas).
  (2) liteparse/pdftotext beating docpluck on real PMC text fidelity was a REAL
      docpluck bug — fixed here.

v2.4.86 — extract_layout._join_chars_with_spaces reinserts inter-word spaces
  from the x-gap. pdfplumber's char stream drops the space glyph on tight-kerned
  PDFs (Cambridge / two-column), so span text glued words ("CNSSpectrums"); the
  F0 layout body then collapsed to token-F1 ~0.00 on 16/30 PMC papers. (LESSONS L-007)
v2.4.87 — F0 (_f0_strip_running_and_footnotes) sources the body from the
  pdftotext text channel and strips identified header/footer/footnote LINES,
  instead of rebuilding the body from spans. Closes the two-column interleaving
  residual at the root (the text channel already serialises columns correctly).
  NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.33 -> 1.9.34.
v2.4.88 — tables/camelot_extract best-effort temp cleanup. camelot-py 2.0.0
  holds the temp PDF handle open on Windows, so the finally-block unlink raised
  PermissionError [WinError 32], swallowed by extract_structured's broad except
  -> 0 tables for every paper on Windows (POSIX/prod unaffected). camelot pin
  capped <3.0. (LESSONS L-008)

Verification: full baseline 1896 passed; held-out PMC token-F1 0.34 -> 0.776
(0 catastrophic, was 16/30 near 0.00); efendic 0 -> 5 tables, xiao/maier PASS.

NOT done (see TODO.md): tag v2.4.88, PDFextractor pin bump, /docpluck-deploy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

(10.1177/01461672251327169). A deterministic render-diff (working tree vs HEAD)
proves all 3 are PRE-EXISTING backlog re-flagged by the known finding-key
case-norm/concatenation bug (memory feedback_canary_gate_nondeterministic), NOT
regressions from this change:
  - diff of all ## / ### headings: IDENTICAL (section structure unchanged).
  - "### Design and Procedure" / "### Power Analysis": separate + clean in BOTH.
  - "### Participants Data Availability, Prer" = canary concatenating the
    "### Participants" heading with the next body line; identical on HEAD.
  - "A positive number indicates an overestimation" table note: duplicated on
    HEAD (lines 58+184), appears ONCE in the working tree (improved).
  - Working-tree render 113k vs HEAD 87k chars: v2.4.88 RESTORED the tables HEAD
    was dropping (camelot Windows bug). The change only adds/improves content.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'prereg_regcheck', 'dev', and 'main' branches. On the 'prereg_regcheck' branch, a "working version of Regcheck" was added, the "reg_check module style" was aligned with other modules, and the "regcheck multipart dimensions format" was fixed to use a JSON array instead of CSV. The 'dev' branch saw the addition of a vignette describing all references, the update of the TUE server to use GROBID 0.9.0, and the implementation of "stat_effect_size" to detect tests via extract_eq() and add implied sample sizes. The 'main' branch had a TUE grobid server added and an email was added by Lakens. Additionally, on the 'dev' branch, the Shiny app was updated with related fixes, and the website was cleaned up by Lisa DeBruine.

repos.summary: Work was done on the 'main' branch. The "docs(todo)" commits marked the v2.4.83, v2.4.84, and v2.4.88 releases, with the latter noting that it has been "shipped" and verified on various platforms. repos.summary:
Work was done on the 'dev', 'fix/186-handoff-no-key-for-non-pdf', and 'feat/213-claude-fable-5' and 'fix/212-cost-estimate' branches.

With a focus on bug fixes, code refinement, and feature enhancements, the team has made significant progress in stabilizing and improving the tool's functionality. Notably, the addition of a sanitizing step and support for Zenodo repositories highlights the project's commitment to interoperability and data integrity. As multiple contributors collaborate to resolve bugs and implement new features, we're seeing exciting connections between metacheck and other projects, with potential applications in data validation and repository management. This progress is a testament to the power of open-source collaboration and we're eager to see the impact of these developments on the broader research community. With metacheck's enhanced performance and usability, we're poised to take a significant leap forward in our shared pursuit of scientific excellence.

files.patch:
@@ -215,7 +215,7 @@ reg_check <- function(paper,
 
   ## guidance ----
   guidance <- c(
-    "RegCheck was developed by Jamie Cummins (<https://github.com/JamieCummins/regcheck>); the local Ollama server bundled in metacheck is inspired by the Ollama integration work of Matilda Gustafsson (<https://github.com/Matilda03>).",
+    "RegCheck was developed by Jamie Cummins (<https://github.com/JamieCummins/regcheck>); the local Ollama server bundled in metacheck is inspired by the Ollama integratio

@@ -176,7 +176,7 @@ without fetching it twice.
 
 The same comparison can run entirely on your own machine. Metacheck bundles
 a minimal local RegCheck server (inspired by the Ollama integration work by
-[Matilda Gustafsson](https://github.com/Matilda03)) together with
+[Matilda Fogato](https://github.com/Matilda03/regcheck-fork)) together with
 [Ollama](https://ollama.com). No text leaves your machine, which makes it

recent_commits.author: Gilad Feldman

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 Package: effectcheck
 Type: Package
 Title: Statistical Consistency Checker for Published Research Results
-Version: 0.6.1
+Version: 0.6.2
 Authors@R: c(person(given = "Gilad", family = "Feldman", role = c("aut", "cre"),
     email = "giladfel@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-2812-6599")))
 Description: A conservative, assumption-aware statistical consistency checker

@@ -1,3 +1,30 @@
+# effectcheck 0.6.2
+
+Exact binomial test reported with Cohen's h. New `test_type = "binomial"`
+matched via `pat_binom_h`, anchored on a "binomial p [op] <pval>" clause
+followed (within ~80 non-period chars) by "Cohen('s)? h = <h>". When a
+"<n> out of <N>" clause is present in the same verbatim, N is recovered
+(`N_source = "binom_n_out_of_N"`) and check.R re-computes the two-sided
+binomial p via `stats::binom.test()` assuming p_null = 0.5 (the most
+common null in binomia

@@ -2922,6 +2922,51 @@ compute_and_compare_one <- function(row,
       paste("Cochran Q reported as a meta-analytic heterogeneity test -",
             "no standard effect size; consistency check is on the reported",
             "p-value vs chi-square(Q, df). I-squared not independently verified."))
+  } else if (tt == "binomial") {
+    # ------ EXACT BINOMIAL TEST WITH COHEN'S H ------
+    # v0.6.2: When the verbatim is "<n> out of <N> ... binomial p [op] X,
+    # Cohen's h = Y", the parser

@@ -726,6 +726,26 @@ parse_text <- function(text, context_window_size = 2) {
   #   homogeneity null with the reported df). The "_<letter>" subscript is
   #   optional; the df may be in [brackets] or (parens) with optional spaces.
   pat_cochran_q <- "\\bQ(?:_[A-Za-z])?\\s*[\\[(]\\s*(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*[\\])]\\s*=\\s*([-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+)"
+  # v0.6.2: exact binomial test with Cohen's h effect size.
+  # Form: "(exact )?binomial p [op] <pval>[, ]Cohen('s)? h = <h>[, 95% CI [<lo>, <hi>]]"
+  # T

@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@
 check_text(
   text,
   stats = c("t", "F", "r", "chisq", "z", "U", "W", "H", "regression", "spearman",
-    "kendall", "kendall_w", "dscf", "cochran_q", "RR", "rdpct", "md_hl"),
+    "kendall", "kendall_w", "dscf", "cochran_q", "RR", "rdpct", "md_hl", "binomial"),
   ci_level = 0.95,
   alpha = 0.05,
   one_tailed = FALSE,

@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+# v0.6.2: exact binomial test reported with Cohen's h
+#
+# Form: "<n> out of <N> ... binomial p [op] X, Cohen's h = Y, [95% CI [...]]"
+# Common in decoy-effect / binary-choice replication papers (CRSP, JESP).
+#
+# Before v0.6.2 these returned 0 rows. v0.6.2 adds pat_binom_h with two
+# anchors (binomial p AND Cohen's h within ~80 non-period chars) plus a
+# NOTE-only check.R branch that recomputes the binomial p via binom.test()
+# when n / N are recoverable from a "<n> out
